rationale: In the citing paper, Nair, Manchanda, and Bhatia (2010) is cited in grouped literature lists on social-effects measurement and as a general reference when labeling some name-choice drivers as peer effects. The paper does not assign this specific citation a distinct role in hypothesis development, model construction, identification strategy, or empirical interpretation. There is no clear direct build-on of the cited paper's opinion-leader setting or asymmetric-prescription framework beyond broad background positioning.
